title: Gaming Laptop RTX4060 AMD R7 8845H 32GB DDR5 5600MHz 1TB

Good for gamers, content creators, and professionals, expanding potential buyer pool

High demand category (gaming laptops)
cons: Asking price is relatively high, limiting immediate profit margin

reasoning: This is a strong buy due to its high-end specifications and excellent condition. While the asking price is not extremely low, the specs (especially the RTX 4060, AMD Ryzen 7 8845H, and 32GB RAM with a 2.5K 240Hz display) make it a highly desirable gaming laptop. The 'like new' condition and 100% battery health are significant positives. There's good potential to resell this quickly for a solid profit, especially if marketed effectively to gamers or content creators. A strategic price point aiming for a 20-30% profit could be achieved.

title: Dell pentium laptop with charger
pros: Dell name recognition
"Top condition" and "great battery life"
Includes charger
Relatively low asking price
Touch screen and 2-in-1 functionality can appeal to some buyers
Windows 11
Seller offers mailing option, widening buyer pool
cons: Pentium processor and 4GB RAM are very low-end specifications, limiting performance and ideal use cases
128GB SSD is small for modern use
Model 3190 is often an older, budget education-focused device
Small 11.6-inch screen may not appeal to all users
Resale market for such low-spec laptops can be saturated and slow
reasoning: This Dell Latitude 2-in-1 with a Pentium processor and 4GB RAM is an entry-level device. While the price is low and it's described in 'top condition' with 'great battery life,' the specifications severely limit its resale value and potential buyer pool. It's suitable for very basic tasks (web browsing, light document editing). You might make a small profit, but it won't be a quick flip due to the niche market for these specs. The [Dell Inspiron 11 3000 Pentium N3710 Quad Core 4GB RAM 128GB SSD](https://www.ozbargain.com.au/node/330876) from 2017 cost $319 new. A refurbished [Dell Latitude 7390 i5-8250U 8GB/256GB](https://www.ozbargain.com.au/node/806013) sells for around $250, indicating even slightly better spec used laptops are not worth much more. The profit potential is there, but the low-end specs mean it will likely sit longer.

reasoning: A HP Chromebook Plus is a decent machine, and the fact it's nearly new with a warranty and sold due to an upgrade are all positive signs. The asking price is good compared to the original price of $469. The profit potential is reasonable, but the market for Chromebooks is somewhat smaller than for Windows laptops. Without specific model details, accurately assessing the maximum resale value is challenging. If the model proves to be a well-regarded Chromebook, this could be a solid pick-up, but it's not a guaranteed quick flip for high profit.

cons: 8GB RAM and 256GB SSD are on the lower side for modern use, especially for a dedicated GPU machine
MX130 is a very weak dedicated GPU, not suitable for serious gaming
Seller ignores offers/trades which can limit negotiation

reasoning: This HP Pavilion offers a decent set of specifications for its price point. The 10th-gen i5 and dedicated NVIDIA MX130 GPU (even if weak) make it more appealing than pure integrated graphics laptops for general use and very light gaming. The excellent condition and relatively recent model year are good selling points. The RAM and SSD are a bit small, but upgradable if desired. There's good profit potential here, but the 'pick up only' and 'ignores offers' might make it slightly harder to move quickly. It's a 'maybe' because the profit margin is good if you can find a buyer who understands the value of a dedicated GPU for general-purpose computing.
